title: rubus × pseudosieboldii
intro: Rubus × pseudosieboldii, also known as Korean black raspberry, is a hybrid plant species derived from the combination of the Asian black raspberry (Rubus coreanus) and the Himalayan black raspberry (Rubus fortunei). The medicinal properties of Rubus × pseudosieboldii lie in its high concentration of anthocyanins and other antioxidants, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming this plant are numerous, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Rubus × pseudosieboldii can be prepared into herbal teas, juices, or supplements, and is also used in traditional medicine in Korea and other parts of Asia to treat various ailments.
